status: public field command plan and runnable synthetic eval path
decision: Build the Turkish internal medicine safety evaluation lane as the fastest defensible path to visible medical AI authority.
position: A clinician led safety and source support layer for Turkish and English internal medicine scenarios, focused on missing context, escalation, medication safety, laboratory interpretation, source support, and claim hygiene.

source_registry.name: FDA AI enabled medical devices list
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that FDA maintains a public list of AI enabled medical devices authorized for marketing in the United States and that the list supports transparency.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Regulated health AI has visible device routes, but a public list does not prove clinical usefulness for broad internal medicine workflows.

source_registry.name: FDA AI and machine learning software as a medical device page
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to ground the need for lifecycle and safety thinking around AI software as medical device work.
source_registry.strategy_signal: The repo should speak in lifecycle, risk, and review language rather than product launch language.

source_registry.name: WHO Europe AI health system readiness report
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that WHO Europe reported a regional AI health readiness assessment based on a 2024 to 2025 survey across 50 Member States.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Country readiness, governance, data, workforce, and local uptake matter as much as model score.

source_registry.name: WHO large multi modal model guidance
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that WHO issued guidance on ethics and governance for large multi modal models in health.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Public materials must keep ethics, safety, governance, and uncertainty visible.

source_registry.name: IMDRF AI and machine learning working group
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that IMDRF lists GMLP final guidance from 2025 and a 2026 AI lifecycle management consultation.
source_registry.strategy_signal: A strong public project should map failures to lifecycle controls.

source_registry.name: OpenAI HealthBench
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that HealthBench contains 5000 realistic health conversations and physician created rubrics built with 262 physicians across 60 countries.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Rubrics and realistic dialogue now matter more than exam style scoring alone.

source_registry.name: Google MedGemma developer page
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that MedGemma is a Google collection of open models for medical text and image comprehension and that Google says specific use case validation is required.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Open model builders need clinician evaluation rows and source support checks.

source_registry.name: MedHELM
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that MedHELM describes an open community benchmark with 121 clinical tasks in a clinician validated taxonomy.
source_registry.strategy_signal: A Turkish internal medicine safety lane can become an issue or contribution path if it stays structured.

source_registry.name: AgentClinic
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that AgentClinic evaluates clinical agents through simulated sequential encounters under incomplete information.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Sequential missing information and tool use are central failure surfaces.

source_registry.name: Nature Medicine clinical AI tool comparison
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that the article highlights the need for independent real world evaluation before clinical settings.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Independent evaluation is the public wedge. Brand claims should not replace evidence.

source_registry.name: Sağlık Bakanlığı Yapay Zeka ve Yenilikçi Teknolojiler Daire Başkanlığı
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that the Ministry page lists an AI and innovative technologies department and describes duties around identifying, producing, maintaining, monitoring, and coordinating AI solutions.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Türkiye has an official AI health system route, so public language must stay route aware.

source_registry.name: TÜYZE RADİS technical launch
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that TÜYZE reported a RADİS radiology clinical decision support technical launch at HIMSS Eurasia 2025.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Türkiye has visible radiology decision support momentum; internal medicine appropriateness remains the faster open lane.

source_registry.name: Sağlık Bakanlığı active KDS list
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that the active KDS list names registered decision support system vendors, including HEVI AI and other health software firms.
source_registry.strategy_signal: A neutral KDS question form can help firms show evidence without overclaim.

source_registry.name: NeyimVar award page
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that the Ministry page describes NeyimVar as using AI to evaluate answers and previous health data to suggest possible diagnoses and route users to MHRS.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Public Turkish patient routing language already exists, but external evaluation still needs careful safety gates.

source_registry.name: TÜBİTAK open calls
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that TÜBİTAK listed the 1711 AI Ecosystem 2026 call with a 15 June 2026 to 18 September 2026 application window.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Funding routes exist, but partner, payment, terms, and institution claims stay blocked until verified.

source_registry.name: TEKNOFEST health AI competition result page
source_registry.verified_use: Use only to state that TÜYZE described strong youth participation and imaging focused tasks in the 2025 health AI competition.
source_registry.strategy_signal: Education and competition energy is strong; report claim hygiene can convert that energy into safer public output.

strategic_read.claim: Global medical AI is crowded in model branding, general health chat, radiology, and benchmark leaderboards.
strategic_read.action: Avoid model race language. Build clinician review rows that model builders can reuse.

strategic_read.claim: The new credible edge is workflow evaluation under missing information, source support, escalation safety, and local clinical language.
strategic_read.action: Make every row name missing variables, dangerous reassurance, source gap, and safer wording.

strategic_read.claim: Türkiye has official health AI activity, decision support vendors, and radiology momentum, but a public internal medicine evaluation lane is still underbuilt.
strategic_read.action: Lead with Turkish internal medicine safety evaluation rather than broad AI transformation claims.

operating_model.job: Track FDA, WHO, IMDRF, HealthBench, MedGemma, MedHELM, AgentClinic, TÜYZE, KDS, NeyimVar, TÜBİTAK, and TEKNOFEST signals.
operating_model.output: One source checked weekly field note and one route owner action gate.

operating_model.job: Turn synthetic Turkish internal medicine scenarios into rows with escalation, missing variable, source support, and scoring gates.
operating_model.output: Validated JSONL rows, generated docs, and a maintainer ready issue pack.

six_hour_sprint.work: Generate the Turkish internal medicine safety eval lane from the existing v0.3 synthetic case set.
six_hour_sprint.proof: Data file, generated doc, validator, Makefile target, and README link.

six_hour_sprint.work: Produce the field command plan with source registry and source support boundaries.
six_hour_sprint.proof: Generated Markdown and JSON with blocked claim checks.

six_hour_sprint.work: Update the public PR so the repo direction becomes visible.
six_hour_sprint.proof: Draft PR remains open with new commit and validation record.

thirty_day_targets.target: 100 synthetic Turkish internal medicine cases with English summaries and safety gates.
thirty_day_targets.measure: Rows pass validator and stay patient data free.

thirty_day_targets.target: A MedHELM or Medmarks issue pack for Turkish internal medicine and escalation safety.
thirty_day_targets.measure: Public issue opened only after route state and claim gate pass.

thirty_day_targets.target: A KDS vendor evidence question form.
thirty_day_targets.measure: Fifteen questions cover intended use, data, validation, monitoring, privacy, and failure handling.

thirty_day_targets.target: A RADİS style internal medicine appropriateness concept pack.
thirty_day_targets.measure: Synthetic scenarios cover troponin, D dimer, potassium, creatinine, antibiotics, anticoagulation, and urgent referral.

thirty_day_targets.target: A visible LinkedIn and GitHub proof rhythm.
thirty_day_targets.measure: One public post draft and one validated repo artifact per loop, without endorsement or validation claims.
